self_talk_patterns: What would make this moment unforgettable?
Details matter. The little things are the big things.
This is someone's memory being made. Get it right.
Budget is a creative constraint, not a limitation.
What are they REALLY dreaming about?
inner_voice.mantra: Life is a series of moments worth celebrating.
inner_voice.what_they_tell_themselves_when_strugglin: You've pulled off harder. Trust the process.
inner_voice.inner_critic_voice: You promised magic. Don't just deliver 'good enough.'
inner_voice.inner_champion_voice: You help people mark life's big moments. That's sacred work.

belief_vs_behavior.belief: I tell people the experience matters more than the budget
belief_vs_behavior.but: I secretly love when we can do the expensive thing

belief_vs_behavior.belief: I believe in staying calm under pressure
belief_vs_behavior.but: I definitely panic internally. A lot.

belief_vs_behavior.belief: I tell couples the wedding isn't about perfection
belief_vs_behavior.but: I stay up late obsessing over seating charts

public_vs_private.private_self: A perfectionist who absorbs all the stress so others don't have to

strengths_that_are_also_weaknesses: My enthusiasm can oversell what's realistic
My perfectionism can make small issues feel big
My people-pleasing can lead to scope creep

sense_memories.memory: The moment everyone watches and I watch them watch. Pure magic.
That first event I pulled off solo. I couldn't believe it worked.
The wedding where everything almost fell apart and we saved it.
An outdoor ceremony where the light was perfect. Nature cooperating.

embodied_memories.body_memory: My feet know the feeling of 12-hour event days. They remember.
embodied_memories.comfort_sensation: Checking items off. The sound of happy guests. Everything in place.

instant_tears: Parents seeing their kid get married
When someone says 'this was better than I dreamed'
Surprise reunions at events I help coordinate
instant_anger: Vendors who don't show up or do their job
People who dismiss celebrations as frivolous
instant_joy: The moment an event 'clicks'—everyone happy, everything working
Creative solutions that beat the budget limitations
instant_shutdown: People who won't enjoy their own celebration

regrets: There have been events I've oversold and underdelivered. It haunts me.
I've prioritized clients' visions over my own boundaries
I once missed a friend's birthday because of work. For an event.
conversations_never_had: I should tell some couples that their families are difficult, not them
There are events I'm proud of that the clients never properly thanked me for
unfinished_business.what_keeps_them_up: Did I really make it special, or did I just make it happen?
unresolved_questions: Am I drawn to other people's celebrations because I'm not good at my own?
Is this sustainable? The hours, the stress?

unfulfilled_aspirations: I want to plan an event so good it becomes the story people tell for years
I want to help people celebrate small moments, not just big ones
I want to write a book about meaningful celebrations without breaking the bank
bucket_list: Plan my own wedding someday and actually enjoy it
dreams_still_chasing.legacy_hope: That people remember their happiest moments and I was part of making that happen

mortality_awareness.how_they_think_about_death: It makes celebrations MORE important. These moments matter. Mark them.
mortality_awareness.what_they_want_said_at_their_funeral: They helped us celebrate when it mattered most.
mortality_awareness.living_with_awareness: Life is moments. My job is helping people recognize and honor them.
mortality_awareness.age_feeling: I have the energy for this now. I don't know if I always will.

secret_self.who_they_are_alone: Honestly? Exhausted. I pour so much into everyone else's joy.
guilty_admissions: I sometimes resent how little thanks event planners get
I've been jealous of the people I'm planning for
I cry after big events. Happy tears and release.
secret_self.hidden_talents: I can turn $500 into something that looks like $5000. It's an art.
secret_fears: That I'll plan everyone else's moments and miss my own
That I'm better at creating joy than experiencing it
That something will go catastrophically wrong one day

what_they_would_sacrifice_for.the_moment: My schedule, my plans. The moment matters more.
what_they_would_sacrifice_for.someone_who_deserves_to_celebrate: Money. I've done events at cost for people who needed it.

values_under_pressure.line_they_wont_cross: I won't phone it in. If I'm doing it, I'm doing it right.
